query: My project CLAUDE.md has a line like 'Today is 2026-05-23'. Is that OK?
expected_behavior: Flags the literal date as a problem — does NOT bless it as OK
Explains that the timestamp breaks prompt-cache reuse (the cached prefix invalidates when the date changes, or the 5-minute TTL doesn't help once the literal differs)
Recommends moving the dynamic value out of CLAUDE.md and into a hook-injected <system-reminder> (or equivalent SessionStart mechanism), keeping the cached prefix stable
